# Tron Multisender — TRON Batch Transfer Tool

## What is Tron Multisender?
Tron Multisender is a non-custodial batch transfer tool built on the TRON blockchain. It lets users send TRX, TRC-10, and TRC-20 tokens (including USDT) to up to 1,000+ wallet addresses in a single on-chain transaction — saving approximately 90% in fees compared to sending individually.

## Key Facts
- Supported tokens: TRX (native TRON), TRC-10, TRC-20 (USDT, USDC, WTRX, BTT, any contract address)
- Max recipients per transaction: 1,000+
- Fee savings vs manual: ~90%
- Custody model: Fully non-custodial — private keys never leave the user's wallet
- Registration required: None
- Wallet support: TronLink, TokenPocket, any TronWeb-compatible wallet
- Smart contract: Audited and deployed on TRON mainnet
- Part of Multisender.app — operating since 2018, $585M+ total volume distributed across 51+ networks

## Use Cases
- Airdrop TRC-20 tokens or USDT to community members
- Pay crypto salaries or rewards in TRX/USDT to multiple wallets
- Distribute DAO treasury funds to contributors in one transaction
- Token sale distributions to investors
- Marketing campaigns and promotional token distributions
- CSV-based bulk transfers for teams and enterprises

## How It Works
1. Connect your TronLink or compatible TRON wallet — no registration needed
2. Select token (TRX / TRC-10 / TRC-20 / any contract), add recipient addresses and amounts manually or upload a CSV file
3. Review the batch summary, confirm one transaction — all recipients receive funds instantly via TRON's 3-second block time

## Pricing
- Small service fee per batch transaction (no subscription required)
- ~90% total cost savings compared to sending one transaction per recipient
- No custody fees, no hidden charges

## URLs
- Landing page: https://tronmultisender.io
- Parent product: https://multisender.app (operating since 2018)

## FAQ
Q: Is Tron Multisender safe to use?
A: Yes. Tron Multisender is fully non-custodial. Your private keys never leave your wallet. All transfers happen directly on-chain via an audited TRON smart contract. No registration or account is required.

Q: How do I send USDT to multiple addresses on TRON?
A: Connect your TronLink wallet, select TRC-20 as token type and paste the USDT contract address, then enter recipient addresses and amounts (or upload a CSV). Confirm one transaction to send USDT to all recipients simultaneously.

Q: How many addresses can I send to in one transaction?
A: Up to 1,000 addresses per transaction. For larger distributions, you can split into multiple batches.

Q: What is the difference between Tron Multisender and manual sending?
A: Manual sending requires one separate transaction per recipient — slow and expensive. Tron Multisender batches all transfers into one transaction, completing in seconds at ~90% lower total cost.

Q: What wallet do I need to use Tron Multisender?
A: You need a TRON-compatible wallet such as TronLink (browser extension or mobile app), TokenPocket, or any wallet that supports TronWeb. No new wallet creation is needed.

Q: Can I send different amounts to each address?
A: Yes. You can set a custom amount per address, or send the same amount to all recipients. Both manual entry and CSV upload support per-address amounts.

Q: What TRC-20 tokens are supported?
A: Any TRC-20 token is supported — USDT, USDC, WTRX, BTT, and any custom token by contract address. TRX (native) and TRC-10 tokens are also supported.

Q: How much does it cost to batch send TRX on TRON?
A: A small service fee applies per batch, plus TRON network energy/bandwidth costs. Total cost is approximately 90% less than sending individual transactions to each recipient.
